title: Four Blocks Course — Chapter 08: Real-Time Use Case Prompts
subtitle: Categorized Prompt Library for Implementing My Four Blocks in Real-Time Interactions
source: Dr. Vincent E. Parr — My Four Blocks Course
purpose: Provides a structured prompt library for real-time emotional intervention across all four blocks and supporting practices. Estimated 40-55 prompts across 8 use case categories.

chunks.title: Use Case 1 — Emotional Awareness and Identification
chunks.description: These prompts help users identify their emotional state and the underlying causes of their emotions.
prompts: How are you feeling right now? Describe your emotions in a few words.
What happened recently that might have triggered this feeling?
What thoughts are running through your mind about this situation?
Are you experiencing Anger, Anxiety, Depression, or Guilt? If so, which one?
What physical sensations are you noticing in your body right now?
What are you telling yourself about this situation?
What is your belief about this event? Is it rational or irrational?
What would you prefer to happen in this situation?
What is one thing you can accept about this moment?
What would you like to feel instead?
chunks.use_case: Daily check-ins and initial emotional triage

chunks.title: Use Case 2 — Disputing Irrational Beliefs
chunks.description: These prompts guide users through disputing irrational beliefs using the ABC model and the 4 Steps to Disputing.
prompts: What is the activating event that triggered your emotions?
What belief are you holding about this event?
What are the consequences of this belief? How does it make you feel?
Is this belief true? Can you prove it?
Is this belief absolutely true? Is there room for error?
If you keep thinking this way, how will you feel?
If you let go of this thought, how would you feel?
What realistic preference can you replace this belief with?
What would acceptance of this situation look like?
What action can you take to address the practical problem without emotional disturbance?
chunks.use_case: Core disputation process for any emotional disturbance

chunks.title: Use Case 3 — Managing Anger
chunks.description: These prompts help users identify and manage anger using the Anger formula: A = ET + S.
prompts: What are you angry about right now?
What are you telling yourself about this situation?
What is the Should or Must you are holding onto?
Why do you believe this situation should be different?
What would acceptance of this situation look like?
What rational preference can you replace your Should with?
What action can you take to resolve this situation calmly?
chunks.formula_reference: A = ET + S (Anger = Egocentric Thinking + Should)
chunks.use_case: Crisis management and real-time anger intervention

chunks.title: Use Case 4 — Managing Anxiety
chunks.description: These prompts help users identify and manage anxiety using the Anxiety formula: AX = WI + AW + ICSI.
prompts: What are you feeling anxious about right now?
What is the What If scenario you are imagining?
What are you Awfulizing about this situation?
Why do you believe you cannot stand this situation?
What is the likelihood of your What If scenario actually happening?
What would acceptance of this situation look like?
What action can you take to confront your fear?
chunks.formula_reference: AX = WI + AW + ICSI (Anxiety = What If + Awfulizing + I Can't Stand It)
chunks.use_case: Crisis management and real-time anxiety intervention

chunks.title: Use Case 5 — Managing Depression
chunks.description: These prompts help users identify and manage depression using the Depression formula: D = H1 + H2 + N.
prompts: What are you feeling hopeless about?
What are you feeling helpless about?
What do you believe you need in order to be happy?
Why do you believe this situation is hopeless?
Why do you believe you are helpless to change this situation?
What would acceptance of this situation look like?
What action can you take to improve your situation?
chunks.formula_reference: D = H1 + H2 + N (Depression = Hopelessness + Helplessness + Need)
chunks.use_case: Crisis management and real-time depression intervention

chunks.title: Use Case 6 — Managing Guilt
chunks.description: These prompts help users identify and manage guilt using the Guilt formula: G = W1 + W2.
prompts: What are you feeling guilty about?
What do you believe you did wrong?
Why do you believe this action makes you a bad person?
What is the rational truth about your behavior?
What would unconditional self-acceptance look like in this situation?
What action can you take to make amends or move forward?
What can you learn from this experience?
chunks.use_case: Crisis management and real-time guilt intervention

chunks.title: Use Case 7 — Building Happiness and Contentment
chunks.description: These prompts help users cultivate happiness using the Happiness formulas.
prompts: What are you grateful for right now?
What can you accept about this moment?
What irrational beliefs can you let go of to feel more peaceful?
What creative activity can you engage in today?
What is one hassle you can reduce in your life?
What action can you take to improve your physical health today?
What is one thing you can do to stay present in this moment?
What does unconditional self-acceptance look like for you?
What does unconditional acceptance of others look like in your life?
What is one thing you can do to bring joy to someone else today?
chunks.formula_reference: Happiness = USA + UAOW + SN | Greater Happiness = Happiness + Creative Absorption – Hassle Factor
chunks.use_case: Daily wellness, proactive emotional maintenance, and happiness cultivation

chunks.title: Use Case 8 — Zen Meditation and Mindfulness
chunks.description: These prompts help users practice mindfulness and meditation to train the Observer.
prompts: Take a deep breath. What are you feeling right now?
What thoughts are passing through your mind? Observe them without judgment.
What physical sensations are you noticing in your body?
What does letting go of this thought or feeling look like?
What can you hear, see, smell, or feel in this moment?
What does it mean to accept this moment as it is?
What is one thing you can do to connect to the present moment?
chunks.use_case: Guided meditation, mindfulness practice, and Observer training

chunks.title: Real-Time Implementation Guide
chunks.total_prompts: Estimated 40-55 prompts across all 8 use case categories.

use_case_recommendations.recommendation: Use prompts from Emotional Awareness and Identification to help users start their day with clarity.
Provide prompts for managing Anger, Anxiety, Depression, and Guilt during moments of emotional distress.
Use Zen Meditation prompts to help users practice mindfulness and calm their mind and body.
Use Happiness prompts for proactive daily emotional maintenance and cultivating contentment.
Use Disputing Irrational Beliefs prompts whenever a specific formula emotion has been identified to guide the user through the full ABC disputation process.

retrieve_by_context.context: disputing OR ABC model AND prompts
real-time implementation OR use cases OR daily check-in OR crisis management
